DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT COLBY CLARK'S MOTION TO WITHDRAW AND AMEND ADMISSIONS; MOTION FOR AN ORDER TO REOPEN DISCOVERY BASED ON NEW INFORMATION THAT PLAINTIFF NEEDS AN ADDITIONAL LUMBAR SURGERY AND TO PERFECT ABOVE. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST TO HAVE THE MATTER HEARD AT 9:30 AM SO AS TO HAVE THE MATTER REPORTED WAS DENIED. ARGUED AND THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO WITHDRAW AND AMEND ADMISSIONS IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. INSUFFICIENT SHOWING OF PRIOR COUNSEL'S MISTAKE, INADVERTENCE, OR EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. PREJUDICE TO PLAINTIFF IS DEMONSTRATED. PLAINTIFF HAS PREPARED THE CASE SINCE APRIL BELIEVING THESE ISSUES ARE NOT IN CONTENTION, AND THE TRIAL IS CURRENTLY SET FOR FEBRUARY AFTER THE FIRST TRIAL DATE WAS CONTINUED. MOTION TO REOPEN DISCOVERY IS GRANTED FOR THE LIMITED ISSUES SURROUNDING THE CLAIM OF NEED OF A NEW SURGICAL PROCEDURE RELATED TO THE INCIDENT. THIS IS WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO MAKING A MOTION TO AUGMENT EXPERT WITNESS LIST. PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; NOT REPORTED.

FOLLOWING ARGUMENT AND RECONSIDERATION OF THE SANCTIONS AWARD, THE COURT CONFIRMS THE TENTATIVE RULING. THE MOTIONS ARE NOT OPPOSED AND THEY ARE GRANTED, INCLUDING MONEY SANCTIONS IN THE SUM OF $840 TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. RESPONSES ARE DUE WITHIN 15 CALENDAR DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE: CURTIS KARNOW. NOT REPORTED.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances on Thursday, February 3, 2011 in Dept. 504:
Scott Bonagofsky, Esq., (Bonagofsky & Weiss, 415.882.1555) for Plaintiff PETER CRANE;
Sean P. Moriarty, Esq., (Cesari, Werner & Moriarty, 415.391.1113) for Defendant COLBY CLARK

DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO WITHDRAW AND AMENDED ADMISSIONS FOLLOWING RECEIPT OF FORMER ATTORNEY'S DECLARATION
The court's tentative ruling: This is not a CCP 1008 ruling. There is no excusable negligence. The request for admissions is deemed admitted. Sanctions are not warranted. Motion DENIED.

The matter is argued and submitted. The court adopts its tentative ruling. Motion for sanctions is DENIED. The court finds this is not a CCP 1008 motion and finds current and prior counsels' actions not excusable negligence. The court finds undue prejudice to plaintiff if motion were granted.

Plaintiff to prepare a form of order and circulate to approve as to form.

Judge Charlotte Walter Woolard, Clerk L. Fong, matter not reported (504)

James Robertson, Reporter: Joyce Long, CSR #8620

Notice Of Motion To Withdraw And Amend Admissions; Notice Of And Deft Clarks' Motion For An Order To Reopen Discovery Based On New Information That Pltf Needs An Additional Lumbar Surgery And To Perfect Above
Off Calendar Dec-23-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT COLBY CLARK'S MOTION TO WITHDRAW AND AMEND ADMISSIONS; MOTION FOR AN ORDER TO REOPEN DISCOVERY BASED ON NEW INFORMATION THAT PLAINTIFF NEEDS AN ADDITIONAL LUMBAR SURGERY AND TO PERFECT ABOVE. Appearances: Scott Bonagofsky of Bonagofsky & Weiss (882.1555) appearing telephonically for plaintiff and responding party; Sean Moriarty (391.1113) for defendant and moving party. Defendant's request to have the matter heard at 9:30 am so as to have the matter reported was denied. Argued and the court ruled as follows: motion to withdraw and amend admissions is denied without prejudice. Insufficient showing of prior counsel's mistake, inadvertence, or excusable neglect. Prejudice to plaintiff is demonstrated. Plaintiff has prepared the case since April believing these issues are not in contention, and the trial is currently set for February after the first trial date was continued. Motion to reopen discovery is granted for the limited issues surrounding the claim of need of a new surgical procedure related to the incident. This is without prejudice to making a motion to augment expert witness list. Plaintiff to submit a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Not reported

PLAINTIFF PETER CRANE'S Motion to Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories and to Request for Production of Documents, Motion To Have Matters in RFA Deemed Admitted; and for money sanctions.
